author: claude
subject: B.7 rem.tape.verify_object: rem-debug archive verify (streaming hash, no restore)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-02-tape-verify-object-b7.md (spec docs/tape-verify-object-design-v0.1.md): rem-debug archive verify (B.7 rem.tape.verify_object), the streaming integrity check. Builds on the committed A.9 archive-read base (4d53b83). Follow TDD per the plan. IN: (1) REFACTOR: extract stream_tape_object<W: Write> + TapeObjectRef + TapeStreamOutcome from run_archive_read's body, and make run_archive_read a thin wrapper (behaviour-preserving; --out file now created before mount; the existing A.9 unit tests must stay green as the regression guard). (2) build_verify_receipt (pure) + ArchiveVerifyArgs + ArchiveVerifyReceipt + run_archive_verify: stream the object via std::io::sink() (no disk, O(block_size) memory — never buffers the whole object), hash the de-tarred payload, compare to --expected-sha256 (the catalog's recorded asset hash, NOT the locator's content_sha256); ALWAYS print one JSON receipt line {verified,expected_sha256,actual_sha256}; exit 0 if verified else 1; --expected-sha256 hex/length decode error path before any I/O. (3) CLI wiring mirroring archive read at EVERY site: RemArchiveVerifyArgs + shared ArchiveVerifyArgs + From impls + RemArchiveCommand::Verify + ArchiveCommand::Verify + tape_target (Verify => Some(library), gating it onto rem-debug) + is_dump_command (Verify => false) + source/format panic arms + the dispatch branch calling pool_ops::run_archive_verify + the two run_archive_tape_command unreachable arms. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. OUT: sidecar parity recovery of damaged blocks; multi-file object selection (--path); bootstrap-digest prefix validation; daemon ReadSessionService verify; the ~/system harness seam flip (bindings.toml + rem.py _real_verify_object) which is SYSTEM-side not remanence; and the manual hardware scenario-B round-trip (Plan Task 4 Step 2, human-run).
summary: B.7 closes scenario-B object verification: stream the tape object and compare its payload SHA-256 to the catalog's recorded hash, WITHOUT restoring to disk or buffering the whole object in memory. It is a near-sibling of A.9 archive read; the only differences are the sink writer (std::io::sink() instead of a File) and the comparison reference (--expected-sha256 instead of the locator's content_sha256). The stricter no-disk/no-memory contract is satisfied structurally because CapturePayloadSink already streams block-by-block (one block_size buffer + the running Sha256). The design extracts a shared generic stream_tape_object<W: Write> so read and verify share the mount/identity/locate/stream sequence rather than duplicating ~60 lines; read becomes a thin File wrapper, verify a thin io::sink() wrapper. Design verified per rust-design-verification with a cargo-check + clippy-clean skeleton (since removed because A.9 was in-flight at design time; the plan reproduces that exact code); clippy too_many_arguments was resolved by grouping library/config/locator into TapeObjectRef. Plan is 3 automatable TDD tasks (refactor, run_archive_verify, CLI wiring) + a manual hardware task.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test passes including new tests build_verify_receipt_matches_and_mismatches and debug_cli_parses_archive_verify, AND the existing A.9 suite (hex_to_bytes_*, decode_locator_*, plan_from_records_*, capture_payload_sink_*, debug_cli_parses_archive_read) stays green as the refactor regression guard. (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: on the akash QuadStor fixture, archive write then archive verify with the correct --expected-sha256 prints verified:true with matching hashes and exits 0; a wrong --expected-sha256 prints verified:false and exits 1.)

author: codex
subject: Implement B.7 rem-debug archive verify
summary: Implemented the approved B.7 streaming verify slice. Refactored A.9 read into a shared `stream_tape_object<W: Write>` helper with `TapeObjectRef` and `TapeStreamOutcome`, so read now creates `--out` before mount and then delegates through the common decode/catalog/mount/identity/fixed-block/space/stream path. Added `ArchiveVerifyArgs`, `ArchiveVerifyReceipt`, `build_verify_receipt`, and `run_archive_verify`, which decodes `--expected-sha256` before any tape I/O, streams the payload through `std::io::sink()` without restoring to disk, compares the streamed SHA-256 to the expected asset hash, and prints the receipt on match or mismatch. Wired `rem-debug archive verify` through the same CLI sites as read, including allow gating, dispatch, and unreachable archive-handler arms. Did not implement parity recovery, multi-file selection, daemon verify, system harness changes, or the manual hardware round-trip because they are out of scope for this dispatch.
verification: cargo test -p remanence-cli (passed; existing A.9 regression tests stayed green)

author: gemini
subject: review: B.7 rem-debug archive verify — PASS
summary: Implementation correctly follows the B.7 design scope. The A.9 `run_archive_read` logic was cleanly refactored into a shared `stream_tape_object` helper, enabling the streaming verify implementation without code duplication. `run_archive_verify` uses `std::io::sink()` for zero-copy/no-disk hashing as required. CLI wiring is complete and mirrors the read path. All tests (including A.9 regressions and new B.7 tests) passed, and clippy is clean. The architecture is sound and meets all requirements.

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 S1: rem-daemon read-only catalog server over a Unix dev socket
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-02-layer5-s1-catalog-server.md (spec docs/layer5-catalog-server-design-v0.1.md): Layer 5 slice S1 — a runnable rem-daemon serving the EXISTING read-only Daemon + Catalog gRPC services over a Unix-domain dev socket, reachable by the rem CLI client. Follow TDD per the plan. IN: (1) remanence-state: add [daemon] socket_path: Option<PathBuf> + socket_path_or_default() (default <state_dir>/rem.sock) + absolute-path validation. (2) remanence-api: add `pub async fn connect_unix(PathBuf) -> Result<Channel, tonic::transport::Error>` (tonic 0.14 UDS client: Endpoint::connect_with_connector + tower::service_fn + hyper_util::rt::TokioIo over tokio UnixStream) and add tower + hyper-util deps (workspace + crate). (3) NEW remanence-daemon crate (add as workspace member): `serve(state: ApiState, socket_path, shutdown)` — tonic Server over a UnixListener, register DaemonServer + CatalogServer, serve_with_incoming_shutdown, remove a stale socket before bind and unlink on graceful shutdown; plus a rem-daemon bin (clap --config/--socket, load_config, CatalogIndex::open (RW once → migrate), ApiState::new_with_config, SIGINT/SIGTERM → shutdown). (4) integration test in crates/remanence-daemon/tests: serve over a temp UDS, seed one pool, connect_unix, round-trip Daemon.health + Catalog.list_tape_pools, assert the socket is unlinked on shutdown. (5) remanence-cli: connect_daemon branches on a `unix:` prefix → remanence_api::connect_unix (keep the existing http path). Register ONLY Daemon + Catalog; deferred RPCs keep returning Status::unimplemented. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. serve()/connect_unix() were cargo-check + clippy verified 2026-06-02 (skeleton since removed; the plan reproduces it). OUT: mTLS/TCP:8443 transport (S2); operations list/cancel/watch + reconcile_tape (S3); WriteSessionService (S4); ReadSessionService (S5); LibraryService + StreamLibraryEvents (S6); Audit (S7); list_files_in_object + get_file (need new Layer-4 queries — catalog fast-follow); changing the default --endpoint (stays http; dev usage passes unix:<path>); and the manual daemon smoke (Task 6 Step 2, human-run).
summary: Layer 5's gap: the Daemon + Catalog service impls exist in remanence-api but NOTHING serves them — no daemon binary, no transport, no socket bind outside an in-process test; the CLI client targets http://127.0.0.1:8443 with no listener. S1 stands up rem-daemon serving the already-complete read-only Daemon + Catalog RPCs over a Unix-domain dev socket. New remanence-daemon crate (serve + bin); a shared connect_unix in remanence-api (the CLI client and the daemon's own integration test both reuse it, avoiding a cli->daemon dep); [daemon] socket_path config. Read-only: each request opens CatalogIndex::open_read_only (existing CatalogService behaviour); the daemon opens the index read-write once at startup to apply migrations; no single-writer owner task (that arrives with S4/S5). The Catalog read surface is already functionally complete — the unimplemented arms are deferred variants (library-scoped / reconcile-from-tape / refresh-from-source) belonging to other slices and correctly return Status::unimplemented. Verified per rust-design-verification: serve() + connect_unix() compile + clippy clean against current HEAD; the DaemonService/CatalogService satisfy tonic's Send+Sync+'static bounds. This is slice S1 of a 7-slice Layer 5 decomposition (S2 mTLS, S3 ops/cancel, S4 write sessions, S5 read sessions, S6 library, S7 audit) recorded in the design doc. Plan is 6 TDD tasks (config, connect_unix, daemon crate, integration test, cli wiring, verification).
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test passes including new tests: daemon_socket_path_defaults_and_parses (remanence-state), connect_unix_to_missing_socket_fails (remanence-api), serve_catalog_roundtrips_health_and_pools_over_unix_socket (remanence-daemon integration test), connect_daemon_unix_scheme_routes_to_unix_connector (remanence-cli). Workspace now has 10 crates incl remanence-daemon. (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: run rem-daemon --config against the dev state; rem catalog --endpoint unix:<sock> pools/tapes returns catalog contents; SIGTERM shuts down cleanly and unlinks the socket.)

author: codex
subject: Implement Layer 5 S1 rem-daemon catalog server
summary: Implemented the approved Layer 5 S1 read-only catalog server. Added `[daemon].socket_path` config with default `<state_dir>/rem.sock` and absolute-path validation; added `remanence_api::connect_unix` using tonic 0.14 over UnixStream with tower/hyper-util. Added the new `remanence-daemon` workspace crate with `serve(ApiState, socket_path, shutdown)` registering only Daemon + Catalog over a Unix listener, stale-socket removal, graceful shutdown unlink, and a `rem-daemon` binary that loads config, opens the catalog index read-write once for migrations, builds `ApiState::new_with_config`, and shuts down on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Added the daemon UDS integration test for Daemon.health and Catalog.list_tape_pools, then wired `rem` daemon clients to route `unix:<path>` endpoints through the shared connector while leaving the default HTTP endpoint unchanged. Did not implement mTLS, ops/reconcile, write/read sessions, library/audit services, list_files_in_object/get_file, or the manual smoke because they are out of S1 scope. `crates/remanence-cli/src/pool_ops.rs` changed only by rustfmt during the required workspace format gate.

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 5 S1 rem-daemon catalog server — PASS
summary: Implementation correctly follows the Layer 5 S1 design scope. The `remanence-daemon` crate was properly introduced alongside the new `[daemon] socket_path` config in `remanence-state`. The `connect_unix` helper in `remanence-api` uses `tower`/`hyper_util` over UDS seamlessly, and the CLI integrates with this via the `unix:` prefix check in `connect_daemon`. Deferred endpoints correctly return `Status::unimplemented`. Socket setup/teardown in `serve()` handles stale sockets and graceful shutdown appropriately. All workspace tests (including the integration `serve_catalog`) passed, and clippy warnings are clean.

author: claude
subject: Layer 5 S4a: single-object write session over rem-daemon (WriteSessionService)
scope: Implement docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-02-layer5-s4a-write-session.md (design docs/layer5-s4a-write-session-design-v0.1.md): Layer 5 S4a — single-object write session over rem-daemon. Builds on committed S1 (3e9ea08). Follow the plan's TDD tasks. IN: (1) Relocate load_tape_by_uuid + LoadByUuidError from remanence-cli/pool_ops to a new remanence-api `mount` module (pub use from api; re-point the CLI callers). (2) pool_write: add expected_content_sha256: Option<[u8;32]> to WriteObjectToPoolRequest + PoolWriteError::ContentHashMismatch; in write_to_selected_tape, after prepare_pool_object (pass-1 hash) and BEFORE the pass-2 tape write, compare and reject on mismatch; set None at all existing constructors. (3) New remanence-api `write_owner` module — the single writer thread: WriteCommand enum (Open/AppendFinish/Close/Abort/Get, each with a tokio oneshot reply) + spawn_writer (std::thread owning the CatalogIndex + DiscoveryReport + StaticAllowlist) + writer_loop using blocking_recv. GLOBAL single-writer: the nested per-session loop holds LibraryHandle + DriveHandle as STACK LOCALS (not a self-referential struct); a second Open while active → FAILED_PRECONDITION; Open does eligibility-only select_tape_in_pool (size 0; real capacity is S4e) + load_tape_by_uuid mount; AppendFinish → write_to_selected_tape → PoolWriteResult.object.to_proto(). (4) ApiState: add write_tx: Option<tokio::mpsc::Sender<WriteCommand>> + with_write_owner constructor (spawns the thread ONCE; Clone must not respawn) + write_session_service vendor; rewrite Open/Close/Abort/Get handlers to dispatch to the thread and await the oneshot. (5) Streaming append_object: first message must be Start; chunks → a bounded PRIVATE spool (create_new in a 0700 daemon dir; cap = declared_size_bytes or SPOOL_MAX_BYTES; exceed → RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED — never whole-object in memory); Finish → AppendFinish command → ObjectRecord; archive_path from caller_metadata['path'] else a default from caller_object_id; honor Finish.expected_content_sha256 (32 bytes). (6) remanence-daemon: discover() + StaticAllowlist from config.libraries + ApiState::with_write_owner + create the 0700 spool dir + register WriteSessionServiceServer in serve(). Pool target only; drive_target/tape_target/recover_session_id/CheckpointSession → Status::unimplemented. Gates: cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. The writer-thread architecture (Send across spawn, DriveHandle-held-across-session borrow, tokio mpsc Sender Send+Sync) was cargo-check + clippy verified 2026-06-02. OUT: CheckpointSession / multi-object atomicity / idempotency-key dedup / ORPHANED-LOST resume (S4b); OperationRef / cancellation (S4c); rollover (tape_sequence/current_tape_index) / per-pool reservation / reservation-rebuild-on-restart / multi-drive (S4d); real capacity vs the 1_000_000-block placeholder + select-before-write no-spanning preflight (S4e); drive/tape targets; the client-supplied-origin-manifest skip-the-pass optimization (S4a does verify-only); and the manual hardware harness e2e (Plan Task 7 Step 2, human-run).
summary: S4a is the write-path anchor: make rem-daemon accept a pool-targeted write session, stream one object in, write it via the SHARED pool_write core (not the in-memory mock), and return the canonical locator, behind ONE writer thread (global single-writer; a second OpenWriteSession → FAILED_PRECONDITION). Key architecture (verified): a DriveHandle borrows its LibraryHandle, so a session can't store both without a self-referential struct — global-single-writer dissolves this by holding library + drive as stack locals in the writer thread's per-session loop. Async gRPC handlers dispatch WriteCommands over tokio::mpsc (Sender is Send+Sync → ApiState stays a tonic service) to the thread (blocking_recv), awaiting oneshot replies; the synchronous tape write runs on that thread, off the async executor. AppendObject streams to a bounded private spool then calls write_to_selected_tape (the spool is required by the core's two-pass source_path contract; bounded+private fixes the Phase-1 flag). Locators match the CLI write path by construction (shared core), preserving the cross-transport parity the read path proved. expected_content_sha256 is honored as a verify-before-tape check (threaded into the core). Findings folded in: relocate the mount bridge cli→api; the write daemon now needs hardware (discover + cap_sys_rawio + allow), unlike the S1 read-only daemon. Plan is 6 automatable TDD tasks + a manual hardware task.
verification: cargo fmt --all clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test passes including new tests write_rejects_expected_content_sha256_mismatch_before_writing (pool_write, VecBlockSink, no write on mismatch), channel_and_command_bounds_hold (Sender Send+Sync, WriteCommand Send), spool_enforces_size_cap; the A.9/B.7 CLI regression suite stays green after relocating load_tape_by_uuid. (Manual, human-run, OUT of Codex scope: rem-daemon --config --allow <serial> on the akash QuadStor fixture; a gRPC write client Open(pool scenario-a) → AppendObject(file) → ObjectRecord; a second concurrent Open → FAILED_PRECONDITION; the returned locator byte-identical to `rem-debug archive write` for the same input; EnumerateObjects finds the object by the same locator_key.)

author: codex
subject: Implement Layer 5 S4a single-object write session
summary: Implemented Layer 5 S4a. Moved load_tape_by_uuid and LoadByUuidError into remanence-api::mount and repointed the CLI. Added expected_content_sha256 support to pool_write, including a ContentHashMismatch error checked after pass-1 preparation and before any tape write. Replaced the old in-memory write-session path with a single writer owner thread driven by WriteCommand messages; Open mounts through the shared API helper, AppendFinish writes through write_to_selected_tape, and Close/Abort/Get dispatch through the owner. AppendObject now streams into a bounded private spool with cleanup on unfinished or consumed spools and honors Finish.expected_content_sha256. Wired ApiState::with_write_owner, daemon hardware discovery, StaticAllowlist setup, 0700 spool directory creation, and WriteSessionServiceServer registration. Kept S4b/S4c/S4d/S4e, drive/tape targets, checkpointing, and the manual hardware harness out of scope.

author: gemini
subject: review: Layer 5 S4a single-object write session — PASS
summary: Implementation correctly realizes the Layer 5 S4a design scope. The `load_tape_by_uuid` extraction to `remanence-api::mount` cleanly resolves cyclic dependencies and allows the shared `pool_write` core to bridge hardware workflows. The `remanence-api::write_owner` thread-isolation mechanism guarantees exclusive device hardware access throughout a session with robust state handling. Streaming `AppendObject` safely buffers to the secure `spool` path until `Finish` is sent. Endpoints outside of S4a correctly reject usage. Tests and clippy are clean.
